headline: KL Building Facade Inspections Reveal 3,200 Defects
context: DBKL mandatory facade inspection found 3,200 critical defects across 1,800 high-rise buildings in city centre.

cards.big: 3,200 facade defects found in 1,800 KL high-rise buildings in 2025
cards.sub: Falling debris incidents from deteriorating exteriors injured 15 pedestrians

cards.big: 280 buildings had loose cladding panels identified as immediate falling hazards
cards.sub: Remediation costs range from RM200,000 to RM8 million per building

cards.big: Average age of buildings with critical defects is 25 years never inspected
cards.sub: Malaysia lacked mandatory facade inspection until DBKL introduced it 2024

cards.big: Singapore requires facade inspection every 7 years for buildings over 20 years
cards.sub: Malaysia has no national requirement leaving policy to individual local authorities

cards.big: Facade defects test whether building maintenance is regulated before people get hurt
3,200 facade defects in KL buildings. Aging towers need mandatory inspection cycles, not voluntary compliance.
